headline: Vertical logic alone misses side paths
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono dropped Lateral Thinking in '70—
Vertical logic alone misses side paths, that's the remedy.
Dig deep in a hole, still miss the orchard next street—
Flip it for today, let the sideways compete.
[Chorus]
Vertical logic alone misses side paths, hear me clear,
You dig straight down while the treasure sits near.
Sideways moves that pure reason won't find,
Train the angle, leave the single track behind.
Logic climbs the ladder, lateral opens the door,
Same problem, new approach, that's what the mind is for.
Stop only digging deeper in the same old well—
Look left, look right, invent a new trail.
[Verse 1]
De Bono said vertical's great for digging deep holes,
But if you started wrong, depth just digs your own role.
Lateral thinking steps sideways—new entry points,
Not better judgment first, but better joint of joints.
Dominating ideas trap the mind in one frame,
You polish the wrong answer and still call it the game.
He showed how patterns self-organize in the brain,
Once a path is cut, traffic pours down the same lane.
So you force a cut across the field—new groove,
Not replacing logic—giving logic a better move.
PO, provocations, random entry as tools,
Not jokes for the meeting—escape routes from rules.
Vertical judges early, lateral generates first,
Then you test the side path so the deep work can work.
History of insight: penicillin mold on a dish,
Not a vertical plan—an accident plus a wish.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: Cursor stuck on one refactor plan?
Step sideways—rewrite the API, scrap the old hand.
ChatGPT loops the same answer? Change the prompt frame,
Ask for the opposite user, flip the constraint game.
Job search vertical: fifty applications, same resume spin,
Lateral: ship a tiny tool that proves you can win.
Kid stuck on homework? Don't only drill the same sheet—
Walk the problem, draw it, teach it to someone on the street.
Standup meetings dig status deeper every day,
But the real fix might be kill the meeting, change the way.
Phone addiction: vertical = more willpower apps,
Lateral = leave the phone in another room, collapse the trap.
Side project stalled? Don't polish the wrong MVP—
Interview three users sideways, rebuild what you see.
Tomorrow: one stuck problem, force three side paths before you dig.
Write them ugly, pick one, ship a two-minute rig.
[Bridge]
Depth without direction is a beautiful trap...
Sideways first, then dig—redraw the map...
The smartest hole is still wrong if it's wrong...
Find the field, then go strong.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own it: logic's a tool, not the only road home,
De Bono taught the side step so your mind can roam.
When the feed, the boss, the model all push one line,
Cut across the pattern—that's the design.
Vertical when you're sure of the hole and the prize,
Lateral when the hole feels deep and the answer dies.
Name your stuck thing tonight—three side paths by hand,
Pick one before breakfast, make it land.
Side paths open—go walk one.
Dig smarter after you change the sun.

headline: Provocation is a tool to escape dominant ideas
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Lateral Thinking—listen close:
Provocation is a tool to escape dominant ideas, not a roast.
PO the crazy line so the mind breaks the chain—
Flip it for today, use the jolt as a train.
[Chorus]
Provocation escapes the dominant idea's grip,
Say the wild thing on purpose, watch the pattern slip.
Not chaos for chaos—structured shock to the frame,
Then harvest the movement when the new paths came.
Dominant ideas feel true 'cause they won before,
Provocation says "what if" and kicks open the door.
PO it, move it, then make it make sense—
Escape the magnet, build the fence.
[Verse 1]
De Bono coined PO—provocative operation,
A signal: this sentence is not a final declaration.
"Cars have square wheels" isn't meant as a fact,
It's a lever to find ideas your habits attack.
Dominant ideas organize everything you see,
So good sense keeps you circling the same tree.
Provocation suspends judgment long enough to move,
Then you follow the movement where usefulness proves.
Humor works the same—sudden shift in the frame,
The laugh is the snap that renames the game.
He taught escape from the "reasonable" trap,
Because reasonable is yesterday's map.
Random word as input, forced connection as drill,
Not genius magic—method you can skill.
Escape first, evaluate later—that's the order of war,
Generate the side road, then score.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: product meeting stuck on "users want more features"?
PO: "what if we ship half as many?"—watch the room re-teach us.
AI draft feels generic? Prompt: "argue the opposite claim,"
Or "write it as if budget were zero"—escape the same.
Dating apps same swipe script? Provoke: no photos for a week,
Voice notes only—see what people really seek.
Kid won't clean room? PO: "the floor is a museum of one day,"
Suddenly sorting becomes a game they play.
Career ladder dominant? Provoke: "what if I never manage people?"
Design a specialist path, not just steeple-to-steeple.
Slack debate looping? Drop one PO line in the thread,
Label it PO so nobody thinks you lost your head.
Tomorrow: one stuck belief, write three provocations by hand,
Pick the movement that feels useful, not grand.
[Bridge]
Crazy on purpose is still disciplined art...
Shock the pattern, then be smart...
Dominant ideas hate the jolt...
That's why the jolt is gold.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So use provocation like a tool on the bench,
Not a personality—an escape wrench.
De Bono didn't sell chaos—he sold controlled break,
So your next move isn't the first one you make.
When the feed, the job, the model all rhyme the same way,
PO a sentence that shouldn't work today.
Write it, follow the motion, keep what can stand—
Escape the dominant, rebuild by hand.
Tomorrow: one PO before you open Slack.
Jolt first, then track.

headline: Deliberately leave the main road of thought
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono in Lateral Thinking laid it bare—
Deliberately leave the main road of thought if you dare.
The highway is efficient till the destination's wrong—
Flip it for today, take the side trail strong.
[Chorus]
Leave the main road of thought on purpose, don't drift,
The default path is comfortable, not always a gift.
Deliberate detour, train the exit ramp skill,
Side roads find what the highway never will.
Main road thinking feels smart 'cause it's fast,
But speed on the wrong route still finishes last.
Step off, look around, then rejoin with new eyes—
Leave the main road, that's how insight arrives.
[Verse 1]
De Bono showed thought patterns carve grooves in clay,
Water runs the old channel every single day.
Main road = the first idea that arrives and owns the map,
Everything after is traffic behind that trap.
Lateral practice is leaving without a guarantee,
Not lost forever—scouting deliberately.
He used techniques: reverse the situation, change the entry,
Fractionate the problem, escape the gentry of "obviously."
Main road says "this is how we always ship,"
Side road asks "what if the problem isn't the tip?"
History: Post-it notes from a weak adhesive fail,
Main road trash—side road made a sales trail.
Leaving isn't laziness—it's trained attention away,
From the magnet of the first good-looking day.
Then return with options vertical can refine,
Detour to invent, main road to design.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: your Notion system is the main road now,
Leave it—one week paper only, see what processes allow.
Figma file stuck? Walk the store, watch real hands,
Photograph friction, redesign from the stands.
Student essay outline too neat too soon?
Leave it—freewrite messy, then cut by afternoon.
Remote team default Zoom? Leave the main road—async doc,
Twenty minutes silence, then talk the shock.
Fitness app same plan forever? Deliberately switch sport,
New muscles, new schedule, new sort of support.
AI autocomplete is a main road machine—
Type the weird sentence first, then clean.
Tomorrow: set a timer, ten minutes off your default tool,
Solve the same task on a side road rule.
[Bridge]
Comfort is a road with perfect signs...
Insight hides where the map declines...
Leave on purpose, not by accident...
Then bring the treasure back intent.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own the exit: main roads are useful, not sacred ground,
De Bono said leave them when answers stay bound.
When the calendar, the stack, the feed all pull one way,
Book a detour on purpose, not "someday."
Name the main road you're stuck on tonight,
Drive one side path before you sleep right.
Deliberate leave—then evaluate what you found,
Rejoin smarter, not just more drowned.
Tomorrow: ten minutes off the default.
Leave the main road—then vault.

headline: Early movement matters more than early judgment
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Lateral Thinking—hit the nerve:
Early movement matters more than early judgment, observe.
Don't kill the seed while it's still trying to sprout—
Flip it for today, move first, sort later out.
[Chorus]
Early movement over early judgment—that's the code,
Ideas need air before the critic takes the road.
Generate first, judge second, protect the start,
Motion creates options, freeze keeps you apart.
Premature verdict is a quiet creativity tax,
Move ugly early, then bring the axe.
Don't score the draft before the draft can breathe—
Move first, then leave or weave.
[Verse 1]
De Bono split generative thinking from the critical knife,
Judgment too early collapses the life of the life.
Lateral mode needs freedom to be wrong in public time,
Because wrong paths often bend toward the climb.
Vertical thinking loves early evaluation,
That's perfect for selecting—deadly for creation.
He taught: suspend the "will this work?" for a stretch,
Let quantity and oddness open a new sketch.
History of design: first prototypes look dumb on purpose,
Judgment later is what makes the purpose surface.
Brainstorming fails when the room scores each line,
Movement dies when every thought needs a fine.
Early movement is not "never judge"—it's sequence right,
Explore the field, then choose the fight.
Patterns form from motion, not from perfect first picks,
So protect the messy minutes—those are the tricks.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: open the doc, dump ten bad titles fast,
Don't delete for twenty minutes—let the pile last.
AI gives three options? Ask for thirty before you rank,
Then judgment has real choices, not a blank.
Kid shows a wild drawing? Don't fix it mid-stroke,
Let them finish the run, then talk the joke.
Standup: "is this idea good?" too early in the room,
Try "give me three ugly versions by noon."
Side project paralysis is early judgment cosplay,
Ship a rough loop, learn from the play.
Email draft anxiety? Write the rude version first,
Then edit to kind—movement quenched the thirst.
Tomorrow: one 25-minute block, no posting, no score,
Only move the work—judgment after, not before.
[Bridge]
The critic has a job—just not the opening act...
Motion first, then the contract...
Seeds don't need a grade, they need dirt and light...
Judge when there's something in sight.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So restate it short: move early, judge late, protect the start,
De Bono's sequence is the creative art.
When Slack, school, and self-talk all demand a verdict now,
Refuse the early gavel—allow the plow.
Pick one stuck thing, run twenty-five minutes of pure move,
No share, no polish, only prove.
Then judge with real material on the table tonight,
Early movement first—set judgment right.
Tomorrow: timer on, critic off for one block.
Move—then lock.

headline: Generate alternatives before evaluating
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono taught it clean in Lateral Thinking—
Generate alternatives before evaluating, stop shrinking.
One option is a trap dressed up as a plan—
Flip it for today, expand the span.
[Chorus]
Generate alternatives before you evaluate,
One idea is a hostage, five is a slate.
Don't marry the first answer that walks in the door,
Build the menu first, then choose what to score.
Evaluation without options is just opinion with speed,
Generation first is the creative need.
List them ugly, list them wide, then pick with care—
Alternatives first—that's fair.
[Verse 1]
De Bono said the mind loves the first satisfactory fit,
Then defends it like a fortress built bit by bit.
Lateral discipline forces more roads on purpose,
Not because more is magic—because choice is a surface.
He trained "how else?" as a habit, not a mood,
Quota of alternatives before the critique can intrude.
Judging early collapses the set to one,
Then you're optimizing a path you never outrun.
Design history: good teams sketch many concepts fast,
Kill most later—that's how the best last.
Science too: multiple hypotheses before the test,
Not one darling theory you refuse to rest.
Generation is work; evaluation is another shift,
Mixing them midstream is how insight gets stiff.
Separate the modes—fill the table, then thin the herd,
Alternatives first is the written word.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: hiring? Don't stop at the first "culture fit" résumé,
Generate five different role shapes, then evaluate the way.
Landing page stuck? Write six headlines before A/B math,
Then test the survivors, not the first draft path.
AI chat: demand five strategies, not one polished reply,
Force diversity, then decide which to try.
Parenting conflict? List three responses that aren't yell or fold,
Pick after the list, not in the heat of old.
Budget cut at work? Generate ten cuts, not the loudest one,
Evaluate impact after the options are spun.
Code architecture? Sketch three designs on paper first,
Then judge complexity, cost, and worst.
Tomorrow: one decision, write five alternatives in ten minutes flat,
Only then evaluate—no earlier chat.
[Bridge]
First ideas feel destined 'cause they arrived first...
Destiny is often just thirst...
Fill the field before you fence it...
Options are oxygen—don't rinse it.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So make one small finished thing that proves the rule true:
A one-pager with five alternatives you actually flew.
De Bono's gift is sequence—generate, then grade,
Not "be creative forever"—be disciplined and made.
When the app, the boss, the algorithm push one best guess,
Refuse the single path until you've listed the rest.
Five options written before any score tonight,
Then evaluate cold in morning light.
Generate first—then choose like you mean it.
Menu before judgment—own it, complete it.

headline: Thinking patterns can be redesigned
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Lateral Thinking—core claim:
Thinking patterns can be redesigned—not just shame and blame.
Your mind's not a fixed OS forever sealed—
Flip it for today, rewire how you feel and field.
[Chorus]
Thinking patterns can be redesigned—that's the hope and the work,
Habits of mind aren't destiny, they're a quirk you can rework.
If the groove is wrong, cut a new one on purpose,
Pattern redesign is skill, not a circus.
You are not stuck with the first road your brain paved,
Train the exit, train the entry, train the way you behave.
Redesign the pattern, then the outcomes shift—
Mind as craft, not as gift.
[Verse 1]
De Bono treated thinking as a skill system, not a talent myth,
Patterns form because the brain loves the path it already with.
Once a pattern wins, it self-reinforces hard,
So "being logical" can still leave you scarred and barred.
Lateral tools are pattern interrupters with structure,
Not positive vibes—operations you can culture.
He showed: awareness of pattern is step one of redesign,
Then deliberate practice builds a different line.
Like learning a language, not waiting for inspiration rain,
Reps of PO, reverse, random input, and restrain.
History of craft: athletes rebuild form after bad years,
Thinkers can rebuild frames the same—sweat and gears.
Dominant pattern isn't always wrong—it's sticky,
Redesign when sticky blocks the next tricky.
You redesign by use: new cue, new move, new review,
Pattern change is behavior you pursue.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: doomscroll pattern at night—redesign the cue,
Phone charges outside the bedroom, new night view.
Meeting pattern is interruption theater—redesign the rule,
One talking stick or timed hats, silence as a tool.
AI dependency pattern: "ask first, think never,"
Redesign: write your take in five minutes, then clever.
Kid's homework meltdown pattern? Change the start, not the lecture,
Snack, walk, then two problems—new architecture.
Manager pattern: only praise speed—redesign the metric,
Reward early options listed, not just the hectic.
Identity pattern: "I'm not creative"—that's a script to edit,
Ten daily side moves prove the edit is credited.
Tomorrow: name one mental habit that fights redesign,
Cut that habit in half this week—draw a new line.
[Bridge]
Patterns feel like personality till you test them...
Personality is often just a system...
Redesign is humble work, not a rebrand speech...
New reps teach what old grooves breach.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own the redesign: pick the pattern that costs you most,
De Bono said you can retrain the host.
When the feed trains reactivity and work trains single-track,
Install a daily lateral drill—build it back.
Half the old habit gone this week—that's the practice plan,
Replace with one side-move ritual you can stand.
Thinking patterns can be redesigned—act like it's true,
Change the cue, change the move, change the review.
Tomorrow: name it, cut it, replace it once.
Redesign the pattern—become the response.

headline: Humor and random input are legitimate methods
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono in Lateral Thinking made it official—
Humor and random input are legitimate methods, not superficial.
The laugh and the random word can crack the seal—
Flip it for today, use the jolt for real.
[Chorus]
Humor and random input—serious tools in disguise,
Not just icebreakers—escape hatches for the wise.
A joke flips the frame, a random word opens a gate,
Legitimate methods when the logic goes straight.
Don't apologize for the silly entry point,
Silly is sometimes the only joint that won't disappoint.
Use the laugh, use the random, then harvest the link—
Method, not wink.
[Verse 1]
De Bono studied humor as sudden pattern shift,
The punchline works because expectations drift.
That same shift is lateral thinking in a flash,
So humor isn't anti-work—it's a cognitive splash.
Random input: pick a word from a book or street,
Force a connection to the problem you meet.
The mind resists, then invents a bridge that pure logic missed,
Random is a probe, not a mystical mist.
He legitimized techniques elites call unprofessional,
Because professional often means pattern-obsessed and confessional of "we've always."
History: comedy writers room methods fuel ad breakthroughs,
Random constraints birth brands and new views.
Legitimacy matters—if the room treats it as clowning,
Nobody uses the tool when the deadline is drowning.
So name it method: timed random, timed humor, then extract,
Keep what works, trash the rest—that's the contract.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: standup stuck? Random Wikipedia page, three links forced,
Connect to the product bug—new fixes endorsed.
Design critique too polite? One intentional joke about the worst flow,
Suddenly people say the real no.
Prompt engineering: seed ChatGPT with a random object constraint,
"Explain this API like a vending machine"—watch insight plant.
Kid bored of math? Random kitchen item as word problem fuel,
Humor lowers threat, learning finds a tool.
Team retro: one funny failure story mandatory first,
Truth arrives easier after the laugh burst.
Writer's block: open a novel mid-page, grab five nouns,
Force them into the scene—new structures, new sounds.
Tomorrow: one small finished thing that only exists if this is true—
A note, a sketch, a prototype born from random and you.
[Bridge]
Serious faces don't guarantee serious thought...
A grin can be the wrench you sought...
Random isn't reckless when the harvest is strict...
Method after the trick.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So stop treating humor as dessert after the real meal,
De Bono put it on the workbench as steel.
Random input isn't magic—it's a controlled collision,
Then you evaluate with adult precision.
When the workplace, school, and apps all demand solemn grind,
Schedule five minutes of legitimate random mind.
Make one finished artifact from a joke or a random seed,
Prove the method by the result you need.
Tomorrow: random word + problem + ten minutes—ship a scrap.
Laugh, link, then map.

headline: Redefine the problem before solving it
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Lateral Thinking—first law of smart:
Redefine the problem before solving it—don't just start.
Wrong problem solved perfectly is still a waste—
Flip it for today, rename the chase.
[Chorus]
Redefine the problem before you solve,
The frame is half the answer you evolve.
Solve the stated issue, miss the real need,
Rename the problem—that's the seed.
Don't sprint at the first wording on the board,
Interrogate the question, then move toward.
New definition, new solutions appear—
Redefine first—clear.
[Verse 1]
De Bono showed problems arrive packaged by habit and power,
The statement already contains the preferred hour.
"How do we make the elevator faster?" might be the wrong ask,
Maybe "how do we make wait feel shorter?" is the real task.
Lateral work reframes: reverse, broaden, narrow, shift the owner,
Change the success metric, change the loner.
He taught: the problem statement is a choice, not a fact of nature,
So challenge the wording like a legislature.
History of product: better "why" kills half the features,
Redefinition saves years of false creatures.
Vertical excellence on a bad brief is elegant failure,
Lateral starts by questioning the railer.
Redefine isn't stalling—it's aiming the gun,
Then solving hard becomes worthy of the run.
Techniques: ask "what else could this be about?" five times,
Write alternate problem sentences before the climb.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: "users don't engage" might be "onboarding lies,"
Redefine, then the metrics and the design revise.
"I need more hours" might be "I need fewer open loops,"
Calendar redesign beats another coffee of groups.
Kid "won't do homework" might be "fear of looking dumb,"
Different problem, different calm.
Team "needs another meeting" might be "decisions lack a owner,"
Redefine, then skip the groaner.
AI "gives bad answers" might be "your prompt hides the goal,"
Rewrite the problem to the model as a whole.
Dating "no good people" might be "my filters fight my values,"
New problem statement, new app behaviors.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour titled with the insight name,
Show up even if the mood's not the same—reframe one real problem in writing.
[Bridge]
The first problem statement is often a symptom...
Treat the symptom, keep the rhythm of the prison...
Rename with honesty, then apply force...
Right problem, right course.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So book the hour: redefine before you build,
De Bono's sequence keeps the work from being filled with noise.
Write the problem three new ways, pick the one that hurts true,
Then solve that—not the polite version of you.
When Slack tickets and school prompts hand you a sealed ask,
Break the seal—question the task.
One redefined problem tonight, one small next step tomorrow morning,
Aim first—then performing.
Redefine, then solve like it matters.
Wrong target fixed is still in tatters.

headline: Separate generative mode from critical mode
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono in Lateral Thinking drew the line bold—
Separate generative mode from critical mode, hold.
Same brain, different jobs—don't force them to duel mid-flow—
Flip it for today, schedule the roles you know.
[Chorus]
Separate generative from critical—protect the split,
Create without the judge, then judge without the jit.
Mixed modes cancel: half invent, half kill,
Full generate first, then full critical skill.
Two clocks, two hats of mind, one honest sequence,
Generation needs safety, critique needs presence.
Don't edit while you invent—that's the tax—
Separate the modes, then relax into facts.
[Verse 1]
De Bono saw meetings where every idea got stabbed at birth,
And rooms where critique never arrived—both starve worth.
Lateral thinking needs a generative window free of veto,
Then vertical and critical tools become the hero.
Separation is time-based and rule-based, not personality type,
Anyone can generate if the room won't snipe.
Critical mode is vital—safety, quality, truth, and cost,
But early critique is how good options get lost.
Writers know draft then edit; de Bono made it general thought law,
For science, business, design—same flaw.
History of labs: brainstorm then analysis gates,
When gates open too soon, invention waits.
He didn't worship ideas—he sequenced respect for both engines,
Generate fuel, then refine the engines.
Mode separation is a practice you can run alone,
Timer for invent, timer for the stone.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: Google Doc comments during the first draft? Off.
Twenty minutes pure write, then comments aloft.
Pair programming: one drives generate, one holds critique for later,
Or switch blocks—don't mix every line as traitor.
Slack design channel: "no judgment hour" labeled clear,
Then "critique hour" with criteria, not sneer.
AI workflow: generate twenty variants, store them raw,
Second pass scores, ranks, and saws.
Parent feedback on kid art: first "tell me about it," full generate listen,
Then one careful craft note—modes don't glisten if mixed into lecture.
Performance review prep: list wins freely first,
Then critical gaps—don't blend into one cursed burst.
Tomorrow: one finished thing that only exists if modes stay split—
A draft file and a critique file, never the same bit.
[Bridge]
The judge and the maker both love you when scheduled...
Together too soon, the maker's strangled...
Respect critique by giving it real material...
Respect generation by making it serial.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own the split: generative block, critical block, hard boundary line,
De Bono's gift is process design for the mind.
When apps push instant reaction and work pushes instant score,
Build a private protocol you enforce more.
Twenty-five invent, twenty-five judge—no post in between,
Make the separation visible on your screen.
Separate modes tomorrow like a professional craft,
Generate full, critique full—don't draft-half.
Modes apart—results together.
Sequence is the lever.

headline: Creativity is a skill with trainable techniques
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Lateral Thinking—myth-bust loud:
Creativity is a skill with trainable techniques—not a cloud.
Not a muse that visits the chosen few—
Flip it for today, practice what you can do.
[Chorus]
Creativity is a skill—trainable techniques, reps and tools,
Not a personality badge for cool schools.
If you can learn a language, you can train a side move,
Methods over mystique—that's the groove.
Stop waiting to feel inspired like weather,
Run the drills, get better together or alone, whatever.
Skill means practice, technique means nameable moves—
Train creativity—prove.
[Verse 1]
De Bono wrote against the genius-only story,
Lateral methods: PO, reverse, random, escape inventory.
He put creativity next to other mental skills you teach,
Not magic—operations within reach.
Talent exists as uneven start points, sure,
But techniques close gaps that myths keep pure.
Schools that only reward vertical right answers miss half the mind,
He argued for deliberate creative practice designed.
History: inventors keep notebooks of method, not only lightning,
Comedians write volume—skill is the fighting.
Trainable means measurable habits: quotas, timers, reviews,
Techniques you can hand to a team and use.
If it's only "be creative," that's not training, that's wish,
De Bono gave the drills so the wish becomes dish.
Skill also means you can be bad early and improve,
Creativity included in that truth you can prove.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: treat creative hour like gym—calendar block,
Not "when inspiration hits"—that's a fragile clock.
Learn three techniques this month: reverse, random, PO,
Apply each to work problems, track the show.
Kid thinks "I'm not creative"? Give techniques like math steps,
Ten alternatives before evaluate—reset the reps.
Team offsite: teach one de Bono tool, run it on a live pain,
Skill shared beats one hero brain.
AI can spit options—your skill is framing, selecting, combining,
Train that human layer—keep refining.
Side project: weekly technique practice logged in a note,
Quantity of drills, not only the quote.
Tomorrow: name one habit that fights "creativity is trainable,"
Cut that habit in half—usually "waiting till able."
[Bridge]
Mystique protects ego and excuses delay...
Skill invites practice today...
Techniques are humble—use them till they stick...
Talent starts, training finishes the brick.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own it: creativity is skillwork with named techniques,
De Bono handed the gym—do the weeks.
Cut the "I'm not creative" habit in half starting now,
Replace with one daily drill you can allow.
Ten minutes reverse-thinking on a real stuck task,
Log it—skill grows when you ask and ask.
Trainable means tomorrow you can be better than today,
Techniques over talent myths—run the play.
Skill up—sideways on purpose.
Train the mind—make the surface.

headline: Parallel thinking beats ego-driven argument
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—drop the thesis:
Parallel thinking beats ego-driven argument—that's the thesis.
Same direction together, not duel for the win—
Flip it for today, kill the adversarial spin.
[Chorus]
Parallel thinking beats the ego fight,
Everyone looks the same way at the same light.
Not me versus you—us versus the problem true,
Argument spends heat; parallel builds the view.
Hats align the mode so pride doesn't drive the car,
Think together, then decide how far.
Ego argument is theater with casualties—
Parallel is strategy.
[Verse 1]
De Bono saw meetings as debate clubs in disguise,
Point scoring, team loyalty, truth as a prize for the loudest size.
Six Hats made thinking parallel: one mode at a time for all,
White facts, red feelings, black caution, yellow call,
Green options, blue process—shared sequence, shared lens,
You attack the idea less when the hat defends the sense.
Ego attaches to positions; hats detach the role from the self,
So you can wear black today without being "the negative elf."
Argument digs trenches; parallel scouts the whole map,
Full picture first, then the decision snap.
History of bad boards: smart people arguing past each other,
Parallel would have forced one hat then another.
He didn't ban disagreement—he timed and typed it clean,
So critique serves the work, not the scene.
Parallel thinking is discipline for groups and for one mind too,
Sequence the modes instead of stew.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: Zoom debate spiral? Call the hat order out loud,
Five minutes white facts only—no opinion allowed.
Slack threads turn tribal? Label messages by hat color code,
Green ideas separate from black risk mode.
Family fight over vacation? Parallel: first facts (budget, dates),
Then feelings brief, then options—no early hate.
Product review: stop "I just don't like it" mid-pitch,
Park red for a slot, finish green first—switch.
Politics at dinner won't vanish, but at work you can install the rule,
Parallel over performative duel.
AI strategy meeting: force shared white-hat data dump first,
Then yellow benefits, then black worst.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour titled "parallel not ego,"
Show up, run one real decision through the modes you know.
[Bridge]
Ego wants a winner; problems want a map...
Parallel thinking closes the trap...
Same direction is not groupthink if modes rotate...
It's full-spectrum debate—on a date.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So book the practice: one meeting, one parallel sequence this week,
De Bono's fix for rooms that only speak to compete.
When Slack, school boards, and startups reward the sharpest tongue,
Install the hats so the quiet truths get sung.
Parallel first—then choose, then own the call,
Ego argument optional, results overall.
Tomorrow: name the next argument you can turn parallel by design,
Same hat, same time—draw the line.
Think with, not against—start there.
Parallel wins the air.

headline: White hat: facts only
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—white first in the kit:
White hat: facts only—neutral data, no spin on it.
What do we know, what don't we, what's the checkable claim—
Flip it for today, separate fact from fame.
[Chorus]
White hat: facts only—numbers, sources, gaps made plain,
No opinions dressed as data, no quiet campaign.
What is known, what is believed, what needs a test,
White hat clears the fog so the other hats invest.
Neutral information is a shared floor,
Without it every argument is war on a trapdoor.
Facts only—then feelings, then risks can play—
White hat first, or you'll pay.
[Verse 1]
De Bono's white hat is the information mode,
Like paper: blank of judgment, full of what is showed.
Include missing facts as facts about ignorance too,
"We don't know X" is white-hat true.
Separate checked fact from assumption and rumor with care,
Label confidence so the room can share air.
White hat isn't "be cold forever"—it's a timed role,
So later red and black don't invent the whole.
He designed it against meetings where vibes replace evidence,
And against data dumps with no process sense.
History of bad launches: opinions outran the numbers,
White hat would have forced the hungers to face the sums.
White also includes who said what and when measured,
Traceability so claims can be treasured or severed.
Discipline: ask for source, range, and date,
Facts only—that's the gate.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: product metric debate? White hat ten minutes,
Open dashboard together—no story spin in it.
Hiring: white hat = skills demonstrated, not "energy vibes" first,
Save culture feel for red, risks for black—rehearsed.
Parenting rumor from school chat? White hat: what was actually said,
Text the teacher, get the thread.
AI answer? White hat: which parts are sourced vs invented,
Ask for citations, mark what's assumed and dented.
Personal finance fight: white hat bank balances and due dates on paper,
Feelings later—facts first as the shaper.
Standup: "status as facts" separate from "I feel behind,"
Two columns, cleaner mind.
Tomorrow: restate white hat in twelve words, run a 25-minute block—
Facts only on one decision, no posting, no talk fluff stock.
[Bridge]
Opinions love costumes; facts prefer light...
White hat undresses the night...
Gaps admitted early save expensive pride...
Know, don't hide.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own white hat: facts only as a practice you can start tomorrow morning,
De Bono's paper mode against fog and performing.
One decision, pure information sheet—known, unknown, source,
Then invite other hats with force.
When feeds flood takes and meetings flood hot air,
White hat is the oxygen you spare.
Twenty-five minutes facts-only—then act on what is real,
Not on the loudest feel.
Facts first—build from there.
White hat—clear the air.

headline: Red hat: feelings allowed on purpose, briefly
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—red on the rack:
Red hat: feelings allowed on purpose, briefly—no attack.
Emotion gets a slot so it doesn't hijack the rest—
Flip it for today, name the gut, then invest.
[Chorus]
Red hat: feelings allowed on purpose, keep it brief,
No need to justify the vibe or write a brief for grief.
Gut, hunch, like, dislike—on the table clean,
Then switch hats so feelings don't own the whole scene.
Suppress them and they leak as fake facts later,
Honor them timed—become a better navigator.
Brief red, honest red, then move along—
Feelings have a song, not the whole throng.
[Verse 1]
De Bono legitimized emotion as information of a type,
Not proof, not nothing—signal you shouldn't snipe.
Red hat forbids the long defense speech,
You don't need reasons—state the reach of the feeling's breach.
"I feel uneasy about this vendor" is valid red,
Then black can find the risk, white can check what's said.
Without red, people smuggle feelings into "logical" claims,
Poisoning white with hidden aims.
He timed red short so drama doesn't colonize the hour,
Feelings matter; monologues devour.
History of deals: ignored gut that later was the warning,
Or unchecked passion that ignored the morning numbers.
Red is not therapy hour—it's a structured admission,
For teams and for self-decision.
Hunches included—intuition as red, not as white fact,
Label it feeling so the process stays intact.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: design critique starts with sixty seconds red each,
"I feel cold / excited / confused"—then teach with other hats.
Hiring debrief: red first round, no justifications allowed,
Then white evidence, then black/yellow crowd.
Couple decision on a move: two minutes red each, timer loud,
Then facts and options—less fight, less proud.
Slack toxicity often is red with no hat label,
Name "red hat" and the tone becomes able.
AI trust: red hat "I feel this answer is slick but empty,"
Then verify white—don't skip the empathy for your own sensing.
Kid refuses practice: invite red first—"what do you feel?" brief,
Then problem-solve—connection before the leaf of advice.
Tomorrow: restate red hat short, 25 minutes obeying it on a real choice—
Feelings named briefly, then other voice.
[Bridge]
Feelings buried become sabotage...
Feelings endless become cabotage of thought...
Brief and deliberate is the red craft...
Name it, don't draft a war from the draft.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So use red on purpose: a timer, a round, a clean sentence each,
De Bono gave emotion a seat without a speech.
When culture says "be professional" meaning "hide the gut,"
Professional is timed red, then other hats shut the glut.
One decision tomorrow: red round first, ninety seconds total max,
Write the feelings, no attacks, no facts.
Then switch—white, yellow, black, green, blue—
Feelings allowed, briefly, true.
Name the gut—then move.
Red hat—prove.

headline: Black hat: caution without permanent dominance
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—black in the set:
Black hat: caution without permanent dominance—don't forget.
Risks, flaws, dangers named on purpose, then you rotate—
Flip it for today, critique without a permanent state.
[Chorus]
Black hat caution—find the faults, the risks, the holes,
But don't let black become the only role that controls.
Caution without permanent dominance—that's the art,
Spot the danger, then still give yellow and green a part.
Critical thinking is a hat you wear and remove,
Not an identity you need to prove.
Name the downside, protect the mission—
Then leave black—new position.
[Verse 1]
De Bono valued black hat as survival intelligence,
Lawyers, engineers, safety—caution is resilience.
But black-hat people often stay black as personality brand,
Killing energy and options with a permanent hand.
The method forces black on everyone for a timed pass,
So risks get listed without one villain in the class.
Then you leave black—yellow finds benefits, green finds paths,
Black doesn't get to own the aftermath alone.
He separated "finding faults" from "being negative,"
One is service; the other is a relative of veto forever.
History of disasters: missing black when hype ran hot,
History of stagnation: black never left the lot.
Balance is the design: full black when black is called,
Full exit when the process has scrawled the risks on the wall.
Caution is a tool—dominance is a culture disease,
Hats fix the disease with timed expertise.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: launch meeting—ten minutes pure black on the PRD,
Every risk written, no sugar, no plea—then switch mandatory.
Security review is black by nature—still time-box it,
Then yellow path to ship with mitigations fit.
Personal anxiety is often permanent black hat mode,
Schedule black: list fears twenty minutes, then green the road.
Code review culture: black comments welcome in a pass,
Then a pass for "what works" so people don't collapse.
Investing Twitter is black-hat addiction with no rotate,
Force yourself a yellow/green block after you update.
Parenting: "what could go wrong" is useful before a trip,
Permanent black makes a kid live in a grip.
Tomorrow: restate the line short, 25 minutes—black list risks, then stop,
Next block other hats—no permanent cop.
[Bridge]
Caution saves lives; caution as identity freezes life...
Wear black, then put down the knife...
Risks listed are gifts to the plan...
Risks as personality unman the clan.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So practice black without staying black—de Bono's precise gift,
Full critique, then the shift.
One project tomorrow: pure black pass, written risks ranked,
Then yellow benefits and green fixes banked.
When the feed rewards permanent cynicism as smart,
Be smarter—timed caution, then restart.
Caution without permanent dominance—live that line at work,
Protect the mission, don't become the smirk.
List the risks—then leave the hat.
Black done—after that.

headline: Yellow hat: deliberate benefit-seeking
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—yellow like the sun:
Yellow hat: deliberate benefit-seeking—get it done.
Not fake cheer—forced search for value and why it could work—
Flip it for today, hunt the upside with trained lurk.
[Chorus]
Yellow hat: deliberate benefit-seeking, not blind hope,
Find the value, the win, the reason we can cope.
Optimism as discipline—ask what good is here,
Even when black was loud and near.
Benefits don't always shout; you have to seek,
Yellow makes the seeking a technique.
Deliberate sunshine after the storm of doubt—
Yellow finds the route.
[Verse 1]
De Bono saw that black comes easy to educated minds,
Yellow needs intention—value hunting of kinds.
Yellow hat asks: what are the benefits, who gains, how might this succeed,
Best-case logic and constructive greed for good outcomes indeed.
Not denial of risk—complement to black's list,
Without yellow, teams only know what they resist.
He made positive assessment a shared duty, not a "pollyanna" role,
Everyone wears yellow so hope isn't one lonely soul.
History of innovation: someone had to argue for the upside early,
While caution listed the burly risks and the hurly.
Yellow includes speculative benefit—labeled as speculative still,
But sought on purpose with skill.
Constructive thinking is yellow's cousin in the room,
Building on ideas instead of pure doom.
Deliberate means timer, questions, notes—not mood,
Seek benefits like a professional brood.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: after a failed sprint, yellow hat half hour,
What did we learn that becomes power?
Feature nobody likes yet: yellow—who might it serve later, what door opens,
Write benefits before you kill it in the open.
Job rejection: yellow—skills proved, network touched, clarity gained,
Then green next actions ordained.
AI tool skepticism: yellow first—where does it 10x a boring task,
Then black on privacy, then act on the ask.
Marriage spat aftermath: yellow—what did this conflict clarify for us,
Benefit-seeking as repair, not as fuss.
Student low grade: yellow—gaps revealed as a map for study,
Deliberate benefit before the muddy shame buddy.
Tomorrow: make one small finished thing only yellow could birth—
A one-page benefit case for something you almost cursed.
[Bridge]
Cynicism feels adult; yellow is adult with courage...
Seeking value is not coverage for trash...
It's completing the picture black can't draw alone...
Benefit-seeking—set the tone.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own yellow: deliberate benefit-seeking as homework for the mind,
De Bono's sun hat when the culture stays unkind.
Pick a half-dead idea tomorrow, force ten real benefits in ink,
Even weak ones—then think.
When meetings only reward the clever no,
Be the process that still asks how it could go.
Yellow after black, before you trash the seed,
Seek the good—that's the deed.
Hunt the upside—on purpose, timed.
Yellow hat—find the climb.

headline: Green hat: alternatives and provocations
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—green for growth:
Green hat: alternatives and provocations—both.
New ideas, lateral moves, creative escape from the stuck—
Flip it for today, grow options, not just luck.
[Chorus]
Green hat: alternatives and provocations on demand,
Energy of growth, not the critic's hand.
How else? What if? Where else could this go?
Green makes the garden of options grow.
Provocations welcome under green—safe to be wild a minute,
Then harvest movements with intent in it.
Alternatives first under this hat's light—
Green is creative right.
[Verse 1]
De Bono tied green to vegetation and creative energy,
The hat for newness, lateral thinking's pedigree.
Under green you generate alternatives without black mid-strike,
Provocations allowed—PO lines that might look like a psych.
Green is where Six Hats meets Lateral Thinking technique,
Random input, reverse, escape—all speak.
He separated green from yellow: yellow seeks benefits of what is,
Green invents what isn't yet—new biz.
Without green, meetings only rearrange the old set,
With green, the option space gets reset.
History of products: green sessions birth the weird that becomes standard,
If black enters early, the weird gets abandoned.
Green needs time and a rule: no shooting till the round ends,
Quantity and stretch as friends.
Then other hats select and harden what green grew,
Sequence saves the new.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: roadmap frozen? Green hat thirty minutes—ten alternatives,
Including one ridiculous on purpose—see what it delivers.
Incident postmortem: green—how else could we prevent this class of fail,
Not only black blame on the rail.
Personal career stall: green provocations—"what if I changed industry,"
Write five paths, then evaluate with other hats free.
Classroom: green for student solutions before teacher answer key,
Alternatives train the mind you see.
Design system boring? Green: steal from games, kitchens, airports—force mashups,
Provocation: "what if our app had no home page"—new maps.
AI brainstorm: green mode prompt—"twenty wild options, no ranking,"
Save ranking for later banking.
Tomorrow: restate green short, 25 minutes pure alternatives—no posting, no score,
Fill the page, then later explore.
[Bridge]
Green is permission with a clock...
Wild is useful when you harvest the shock...
Alternatives are oxygen for choice...
Provocation is a voice.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So wear green on purpose when stuck feels like fate,
De Bono's growth hat opens the gate.
One stuck problem tomorrow: ten alternatives + one provocation line,
Then switch hats to refine.
When culture rewards safe incremental only,
Schedule green so the future isn't lonely.
Alternatives and provocations—practice till they're easy tools,
Green hat—break the rules that rule the fools.
Grow options—then choose clean.
Green first—then the machine.

headline: Blue hat: process control
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—blue like the sky that organizes weather:
Blue hat: process control—hold the sequence together.
Who facilitates the thinking, not just the content fight—
Flip it for today, manage the mode, not only who's right.
[Chorus]
Blue hat: process control—agenda for the mind,
Which hat when, how long, what we leave behind.
Meta-thinking: think about the thinking in the room,
Blue keeps the orchestra from noise and gloom.
Without blue, hats become costumes for chaos,
With blue, parallel thinking actually got us.
Call the switches, summarize, decide the next—
Blue hat runs the text.
[Verse 1]
De Bono's blue hat is the conductor and the map,
Defining focus, tools, timeboxes, and the wrap.
Blue opens: "we're here to decide X with hats in this order,"
Blue closes: "we agreed Y, next action, recorder."
Anyone can wear blue, but someone must, or process decays,
Default is argument and foggy days.
Blue also monitors: "we're slipping into black during green,"
Redirects the room to the mode on the screen.
It's not bossiness—it's thinking design for groups,
And solo: blue is your personal protocol of loops.
History of great facilitators is blue-hat skill under other names,
De Bono made it explicit for thinking games.
Blue chooses when to invite red, when white is enough,
When green needs more time, when black was rough and still needed tougher stuff—or less.
Process control is respect for people's limited attention,
Blue is the prevention of invention's detention by mess.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: standups bleeding into debates—blue hat owner rotates,
Two minutes status white, then one green ask—gates.
Remote workshop: blue posts the hat order in the Zoom chat first,
Timer bot enforces, no ego burst mid-green thirst.
Solo deep work: blue hat morning note—"today white research then green draft,"
Process control for yourself when apps draft your attention aft.
Family meeting: blue parent or teen runs the agenda fair,
Feelings red timed, decisions listed with care.
Incident command is blue under stress—roles clear, order clear,
Thinking process under fear.
AI multi-step projects: blue prompt as project manager—
"First outline process, then execute hats in order"—better.
Tomorrow: make one small finished thing that proves blue—
A one-page process card for your next real meeting, true.
[Bridge]
Content is sexy; process is survival...
Blue is the quiet rival of chaos...
Control the thinking method, free the minds...
Blue designs the kinds.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own blue hat: process control as a craft you can practice tomorrow,
De Bono's sky over the hats you borrow.
Write the sequence before the meeting starts,
Timeboxes, owner, outputs—those parts.
When Slack and calendars invent chaos by default,
Blue is how adults manage thought, not just vault results.
One meeting, one blue owner, one published hat order tonight prepared,
Run it clean—be rare.
Process first—then content sings.
Blue hat—run the strings.

headline: One hat at a time in groups
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—group rule gold:
One hat at a time in groups—or the method folds.
All look the same direction in the same minute—
Flip it for today, stop mixed-mode infinite.
[Chorus]
One hat at a time in groups—parallel, not soup,
Everybody white, then everybody red—group loop.
Mixed hats in one moment is just old debate,
Same mode together is the method's fate.
Don't let half the room black while half green dreams,
Sequence the colors, share the beams.
One hat, all heads—then switch on the call—
That's how meetings stand tall.
[Verse 1]
De Bono insisted the power is parallel synchrony,
If modes mix, you recreate adversarial symphony.
One person green while another black is a duel by another name,
The hat system fails if you ignore the frame.
Group discipline: facilitator (blue) announces the hat,
Everyone contributes in that mode—full stop, that's that.
Even quiet people get a lane when the mode is shared,
Loud black can't drown green if green is the only air.
He designed it for corporate rooms drowning in style clashes,
One-hat rounds turn clashes into passes through the data.
History of workshops: best ones constrain the speaking type,
Worst ones free-for-all until the night is ripe with nothing decided.
One hat at a time is fairness and focus in one rule,
Not bureaucracy—oxygen for the pool.
Practice until the switch is muscle memory for the team,
Then speed increases—same scheme.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: product triad meeting—sticky notes by color rounds,
No cross-color talk until the round compounds.
Discord or Slack: channel topics as hat rounds with time stamps,
"White hat only until :15"—reduce the flame wars' amps.
Classroom group work: teacher calls hat changes out loud,
Kids learn modes, not just content proud.
Board Zoom: breakout is optional; better is full-room one hat,
Then switch—cameras on for red, docs for white—like that.
Couple planning finances: both wear white together first,
Not one budgeting black while one dreams yellow worst.
Game design jam: whole table green for twenty, then black together,
One hat multiplies the tether.
Tomorrow: make one small finished thing—a meeting card: hat order + times,
Use it once, prove the rhymes.
[Bridge]
Mixing modes feels natural and costs clarity...
One mode feels artificial and buys parity...
Trust the awkward switch—it gets smooth...
Groups need the groove.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So enforce one hat at a time like a sport has rules that free the play,
De Bono's group law for a better day.
Next group decision: announce the hat, all in, timer on,
No mixed-mode freestyle till the sequence is gone.
When hybrid work multiplies crosstalk and ego,
One hat is how you make thinking go.
Print the order, run the rounds, decide at the end with blue,
One hat—see it through.
All same direction—then switch.
Groups that can do that—rich.

headline: Hats separate ego from idea
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—deepest social gift:
Hats separate ego from idea—detach the lift.
You can attack the black view without attacking the person wearing pride—
Flip it for today, keep dignity, still decide.
[Chorus]
Hats separate ego from idea—that's the release,
Critique the mode, not the human peace.
I'm not "the negative one"—I'm wearing black this round,
Tomorrow green—I'm not forever bound.
Identity off the idea so learning can live,
Defend less, explore more—what the hats give.
Ego unhooked from the thought on the table—
Safer rooms, more able.
[Verse 1]
De Bono watched people marry their first position,
Then defend it as self-worth in a verbal mission.
Hats let you contribute risks without becoming "Dr. No,"
And contribute wild ideas without being the office show.
Because everyone rotates, no one owns a permanent faction,
Ego finds less traction for attachment action.
"The black hat says..." is different language than "you always kill ideas,"
Process language lowers the spears.
He designed psychological safety before the buzzword era,
Through structure, not posters near the coffee mirror.
History of toxic cultures: idea = identity = status game,
Hats break the game by changing the frame.
You can reverse your view when the hat changes—no loss of face,
Flexibility becomes grace, not disgrace.
Separate ego from idea and truth gets a longer runway,
People update without the ego gunplay.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: performance culture on LinkedIn attaches self to hot takes,
At work, use hats so takes can break without heartbreaks.
Code review: "black hat on this API" not "your code is bad,"
Language that separates ego—less sad.
Teen arguments: "red hat feelings" vs "you're dramatic,"
Family systems heal when roles aren't static.
Political office Slack—harder—but product teams can model the split,
Idea critique without identity hit.
AI debate tools: assign hats so students argue modes not tribes,
Practice detachment with vibes.
Habit that fights this: always defending your first Slack reply—
Cut that habit in half this week—let replies die and revise.
Tomorrow: name the ego-attachment habit, cut it half,
Wear a different hat on purpose in the next staff.
[Bridge]
Ego wants to win; learning wants to update...
Hats make update a legitimate state...
You're not your last opinion in the thread...
Detach—stay well-fed.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So practice separation: next critique, name the hat not the human,
De Bono's gift when rooms get inhuman.
Cut the habit of defending first drafts like your name is on trial,
Revise under a new hat—new dial.
When status games rule startups and schools alike,
Hats are a small technology of unlike fighting like.
Ego off the idea—try it once tomorrow in a real room,
Watch the air leave the doom.
Separate—then think freer.
Hats on—ego clearer.

headline: Meetings become constructive when roles rotate
song:
Yo, Edward de Bono, Six Thinking Hats—closing principle strong:
Meetings become constructive when roles rotate along.
Nobody stuck as permanent optimist or permanent critic—
Flip it for today, rotate the civic of thinking, make it stick.
[Chorus]
Meetings turn constructive when roles rotate,
Same people, new angles—that's the update.
Fixed roles calcify into politics and silence,
Rotation gives every voice full science.
Wear black, then yellow, then green in turn,
Full spectrum thinking—watch the room learn.
Rotate the hats, rotate the power to frame—
Constructive is the name of the game.
[Verse 1]
De Bono saw fixed meeting roles: the naysayer, the cheerleader, the boss,
Each trapped, each performing for the loss of full thought.
Rotation forces breadth: the quiet analyst must green,
The visionary must black—growth of the scene.
Constructive means building toward a decision with full map coverage,
Not a win for a faction's coverage.
When roles rotate, listening improves because you'll need that mode later,
Empathy becomes structural, not a debater's theater.
He tied rotation to fairness and to quality of output both,
Diversity of thought without hiring an oath of new people—same crew, new growth.
History of agile rituals that work: rotating facilitators, rotating scribes,
Hats extend that to thinking tribes.
Without rotation, hats become labels stuck on persons forever,
Which recreates the problem the system endeavors to sever.
Rotate on purpose every meeting or every round inside,
Motion is how constructive meetings survive.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: weekly product sync—hat order posted, roles rotate who blue-leads,
Intern runs blue next week—leadership seeds.
Remote all-hands breakout: each person must contribute once per hat round,
Rotation kills the same three voices crowned.
School group projects: assign rotating hat captains by day,
Constructive collaboration, less free-rider play.
Family Sunday planning: kids take blue process control once a month,
Roles rotate, respect grows once.
Board meetings: independent directors lead different hat segments,
Less CEO theater, more segments of true sense.
Even 1:1s: manager and report switch who wears black first alternate weeks,
Power softens, truth peaks.
Tomorrow: restate the insight short, run one 25-minute meeting block that rotates—
Two hats minimum, roles move, no posting the flex.
[Bridge]
Fixed roles feel efficient and become prisons...
Rotation feels slower and builds decisions...
Constructive is a verb you schedule...
Rotate—miracles of the practical.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So make meetings constructive by rotating roles like de Bono drew,
Not longer meetings—better who does who.
Next calendar event: publish hat order and rotation before you start,
Blue leads change hands—that's the art.
When hybrid work freezes the same dynamics on screen,
Rotation is the intervention clean.
One meeting tomorrow, roles rotate, decision written at the end,
Constructive—defend the method as friend.
Rotate—build—decide.
Hats move—meetings thrive.
